In this full tutorial, I take a standard SharePoint Asset Management list form and transform it into a modern Power Apps experience entirely using AI. Every enhancement—from the layout and styling to the navigation and Power Fx—is generated through natural language prompts using the Canvas

In this video, you'll get a practical walkthrough of how Microsoft's Copilot ecosystem is licensed today, including what is free, what requires a Microsoft 365 Copilot license, and which products now consume AI credits.

You'll learn:
• What you get with free Copilot Chat included with Microsoft 365
• What the Microsoft 365 Copilot license unlocks (including Work IQ)
• How Copilot Cowork licensing works and w

videos.title: GLM 5.2 is great ... but #AI #GLM #Claude #OpenAI #Anthropic
videos.description:
GLM 5.2 is genuinely, seriously good. It is free, and on most real work it matches or beats Claude. On paper, this is the model everyone should be running by Monday.

But. There are engineers spending eighty thousand dollars a week in tokens who could cut that bill overnight, and they are not switching. Anthropic and OpenAI keep growing revenue like GLM does not exist. That is the puzzle.

I broke down why GLM 5.2 keeps losing this fight despite the price here: https://nateszerotoai.substack.com

videos.title: You can build your AI's memory just by talking. Here's the catch. #AI #aiagents #AImemory
videos.description:
A few months ago you could not do this. Today you can sit down, talk to the agent already on your computer, and it will build most of your own memory stack for you. I put it around eighty percent, and I have compared notes with other people building the same thing, so that is not a wild guess. The jump from February to now is genuinely wild.

Here is the catch, and it is the whole reason the stack matters. An agent that is this good at acting on your intent will also act on the wrong version of

videos.title: This Skill Turns Claude Into a Web Design MONSTER
Claude Code Masterclass for People Who Don’t Code
videos.description: Claude Code is one of the most powerful tools for business automation, but it can be intimidating if you don't have a coding background. This is the complete guide for people who don't code, updated for 2026. Forget the confusing jargon; we're giving you the 20% of Claude Code's features that will deliver 80% of the value, all in plain English.

videos.title: How the Escalating AI Wars Benefit You
videos.description:
Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI signals an AI race expanding beyond models into hardware, efficiency and control. NLW explains how the escalating competition is producing better models, higher usage limits and lower costs for users—and why the opportunity may not last. In the headlines: the White House weighs action on Chinese open-source AI and the UAE gains greater access to advanced US chips.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI. 
Subscribe to t

videos.title: How to Help People Thrive with AI
videos.description:
AI can eliminate tedious work, but its real promise is helping people stretch their capabilities and pursue things that weren’t possible before. From the risk of “AI brain fry” to Uber’s agentic pods, NLW explores how organizations can turn productivity gains into human growth and entirely new forms of work.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI. 
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen: https://pod.link/16806336

videos.title: Anthropic Can Now Read Claude's Mind
videos.description:
UN leaders called for a ban on autonomous weapons and introduced a child safety pledge to hold AI developers accountable. Anthropic revealed a J-Lens tool to read and edit Claude's private reasoning, exposing a small "J-space" of reportable concepts driving model decisions. Policy and market moves included Illinois' new AI audit law, China's chatbot limits, rapid growth in training-data firms, and debate over NVIDIA hardware timelines.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important

items.title: Planner: Planner Agent chat in Basic Plans
items.description: Introducing built-in Planner Agent chat with natural language Q&A, smart task discovery, and in-plan task management.

items.title: SharePoint: Enterprise store plugins in Copilot in SharePoint
items.description:
Copilot in SharePoint now supports plugins from your enterprise store. A plugin can bundle skills, MCP servers, and other capabilities that extend what Copilot can do. Any plugin made available to your organization, whether installed tenant-wide by an admin or added by you, works directly in Copilot in SharePoint, all governed by your M365 admin.

For example, your organization can publish a company-wide accessibility plugin once and have it work consistently across all agentic products, includi

items.title: SharePoint: Personal Skills in Copilot in SharePoint
items.description: Create your own personal skills in Copilot in SharePoint and use them anywhere you work. Personal skills travel with you across SharePoint sites and OneDrive—so a skill like document generation works everywhere without recreating it per site. You own your skills, saved as a markdown file in your OneDrive.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Copilot Analytics - Agent 365 Dashboard in Insights
items.description: Customers can now access their Agent 365 data through the Agent Dashboard in Copilot Analytics (Insights), enabling leaders to understand activity across all agents registered through Agent 365. Customers with Agent 365 and Copilot will now see the Agent 365 Dashboard with the ability to view historical data in Agent Dashboard.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: PowerPoint Live Light theme support
items.description: Presenting PowerPoint slides in Teams just got better. PowerPoint Live now supports light mode, giving presenters the option to present in light, dark, classic, or high contrast mode based on their preferences and system settings.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Expand presenter visibility in 'Manage what attendees see' for Teams events
items.description: Users will now be able to resize and expand the presenter panel in 'Manage what attendees see' options for Teams events. This feature helps make it easier to view, manage, and switch between presenters during events.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Teams events attendance and engagement report policy
items.description: Teams events will soon have a separate attendance and engagement report policy from the "CsTeamsMeetingPolicy" attendance and engagement report policy. This will permit admins to allow Teams events organizers to access attendance and engagement reports, while restricting access to Teams meeting attendance and engagement reports.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Enhanced live captions settings and right-side panel view
items.description:
We are improving the live captions experience in Microsoft Teams with new viewing layouts and streamlined configuration options. Users can now view live captions in a new right-side panel, in addition to the existing options of viewing them at the top or bottom of the screen, or popping them out into a separate window.
We are also making it easier to manage caption settings directly within the meeting. By clicking the existing gear icon in the live captions pane, users will see a consolidated me

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Multilingual recap available with translation button
items.description: After meeting recap is automatically generated in the translation language chosen during the meeting, users can continue to change the language of the meeting recap after its generated.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: View and manage auto attendant shared voicemails directly in the Queues app
items.description: Calls to an Auto Attendant that are routed to voicemail will now appear directly within Queues app. This enables organizations to manage shared Auto Attendant voicemails in a centralized experience, making it easier for teams to track, triage, and respond to customer messages.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Prevent presence transition to "Do not disturb" setting when screen sharing (GCC High, DoD)
items.description: We're introducing a setting that allows users to opt-out of transitioning presence automatically to Do Not Disturb when presenting or screen sharing. This ensures that they can continue to receive calls, keeping them connected and responsive without manual presence adjustments.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: External presenters can access production controls for Teams events
items.description: Organizations can allow external presenters to manage Teams events by granting them access to production controls. Federated (B2B) and guest users can be specified in the "Who has control of production tools" setting, enabling trusted external presenters to run events and manage what attendees see. This feature supports organizations that work with third-party event producers and vendors.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Meeting recap app
items.description: The meeting recap app brings all your meeting recaps into one place, making it easier to find, filter, and catch up. Quick filters help you surface the right meetings instantly, while audio recap lets you efficiently review multiple meetings at once. You can also browse and revisit any recap from the past 30 days.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Lifecycle Management - Insights and policy recommendations on sensitive M365 data for better Data Security and Compliance posture
items.description: Insights on sensitive M365 data (OneDrive and SharePoint) and recommendations on retention policies for customers to better govern their sensitive data.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Lifecycle Management – Insights and policy recommendations on Microsoft Copilot and AI Apps interactions for better Data Security and Compliance posture
items.description: Provide insights on Copilots and AI Apps usage and recommend retention policies for customers to govern their Copilots and AI App interactions.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Security Investigations – Introducing OCR support
items.description: Microsoft Purview Data Security Investigations (DSI) is extending our investigations by adding optical character recognition (OCR), extracting text from images and incorporating it into investigation data. This enables AI-powered deep content analysis to uncover data security risks that are often hidden within visual content.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Endpoint Data Loss Prevention: Add control to include or exclude Default file paths exclusions for Windows
items.description: Previously customers would have system files either included or excluded manually configured. With this feature there is now a capability to provide this by default and have the option of including or excluding those paths. This list is managed by Microsoft to ensure accuracy and ease of management.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Viva Glint - Copilot assisted commenting in Glint survey taking
items.description: Viva Glint survey takers will be able to use a streamlined version of Microsoft Copilot in the Glint survey taking experience to help them rewrite their comments, helping survey takers feel more confident about the feedback they’re providing. By increasing perceived security through reducing identifiable writing patterns and minimizing the effort required to refine feedback, this experience can encourage greater participation, longer responses, and more candid comments, which can drive higher en

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Report external users for security concerns in Teams
items.description: Users can now report suspicious external users directly within Teams, alongside existing block actions. Reports are surfaced in the Teams admin center, giving admins visibility into potentially risky interactions and enabling them to investigate and take appropriate actions. This enhancement helps organizations respond faster to phishing, impersonation, and other external threats while leveraging end-user signals as an additional layer of protection.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Endpoint Data Loss Prevention - Add support of hyperlinks in warn & block toast messages for Edge browser
items.description: With this feature, data officers can now complete their coverage story by now embedding hyperlinks within toast messages for the Edge browser. When this rule is triggered, the end user will see a toast with a customized title and message with a hyperlink. This feature is useful when organizations need to direct users to a specific resource or a repository for more specific instruction (e.g. internal policy SharePoint site).

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Block External Domain/User action in DLP for SharePoint/OneDrive
items.description: Block External Domain/User action in Microsoft Purview DLP for SharePoint and OneDrive, gives organizations stronger control over who can access sensitive files. With this enhancement, admins can configure DLP policies that block file access for specific external domains or individual users. This capability helps prevent unintended exposure by external users and supports enterprise compliance requirements.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Lifecycle Management - Power Automate integration with records management
items.description: Initiate a Power Automate workflow when the item reaches the end of its retention period. For example, execute a complex disposition approval process or move the file to an archive.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: New Post Creation Experience in Viva Enage
items.description: In Viva Engage, we've simplified how to create posts. The new post creation experience makes it easier and faster to share in Viva Engage with a cleaner, more intuitive publishing experience. It brings clearer layout, better accessibility, and smarter organization of tools—so you can focus on creating great posts without extra clicks or clutter.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Information Protection-Content Explorer – Enhanced Tag (SIT, Labels) Filter at UI Layer
items.description: Introduces enhanced controls in Content Explorer’s UI, enabling scoped visibility and filtering of SIT and label data. This will help with content discoverability and governance.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Natural language support for Scheduling prompts
items.description: This feature allows users to simply express their intention conversationally, and Copilot infers their intention and creates a scheduled prompt automatically. We are unfortunately unable to move forward with this feature. We apologize for the inconvenience.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Purview Information Protection | Classifier Simulation Mode (Health Monitoring)
items.description:
Microsoft Purview introduces the Classifier Simulation Mode, the first phase of the broader Classifier Health Monitoring Platform. 
 
This capability enables you to test, analyze, and optimize custom classifiers on production data before publishing them, hence offering a reliable means of verifying the effectiveness of classifiers before committing them to live environments.  

Custom classifiers are powerful tools but can sometimes include inefficient regex patterns or overly broad logic, leadi

items.title: Microsoft Purview compliance portal: Data Lifecycle Management - Apply a retention policy to Microsoft Planner content
items.description: Introducing Data lifecycle management capabilities for Microsoft Planner. Compliance admins can now create a retention policy to manage content located in Microsoft Planner such as tasks for plans associated with an Microsoft 365 Group.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Copilot Analytics - Insights in Cowork and WorkIQ spend and usage
items.description: Insights into AI spend (Copilot credit usage) at a group or team level will be available in Viva Insights as a dashboard and in Advanced insights. The services in scope of this release are Cowork and Work IQ API. The dashboard experience is available to managers with at least 5 direct reports (scoped access), the Insights analysts, and Global administrators and is enabled by default to ensure immediate access to usage-based cost insights. For users to be able to use Cowork or WorkIQ API, your te

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Information Protection - Rights Management connector – Certificate-based authentication
items.description: The Microsoft Rights Management (RMS) connector is moving from shared-secret authentication to certificate-based authentication, improving its security posture. With this update, administrators configure their own Microsoft Entra app registration and certificate, then use the new PowerShell module to configure the certificate for each workload (Connector, Exchange, SharePoint, and FCI). New PowerShell cmdlets handle certificate import, registry configuration, private-key permissions, and validat

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention - Granular classification error controls (Exchange online)
items.description: Granular Protections for Exchange Online is available in public preview. This release gives administrators fine-grained control to create DLP policies based on specific classification or text extraction failure types — such as timeout, throttling and other scan errors — rather than one blanket policy for all failure conditions.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: eDiscovery – Enhancement of Loop and Copilot Pages in Review and Export
items.description: Improves eDiscovery workflows for Loop and Copilot pages by enabling full indexing of page content for keyword search within review sets and adding HTML format support for export from search.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Security Investigations – notification and improved search capabilities
items.description: We're introducing notification capabilities and search improvements to Microsoft Purview Data Security Investigations. Investigators will receive notifications when key investigation events occur, such as when AI jobs complete or when new data is added to scope. Search improvements include enhanced query building to more easily identify potentially impacted data

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Copilot Chat can now better match search results from Scanned PDFs and from text inside images embedded in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
items.description: Copilot Chat can now better match search results from Scanned PDFs and from text that appears inside images embedded in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint files — such as scanned contracts in Word, screenshots of tables in Excel, or whiteboard photos in PowerPoint — content that was previously difficult to search and find.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Entra passwordless resource account support for Teams Rooms on Windows devices
items.description: Teams rooms on Windows supports Entra Resource Accounts for secure, passwordless sign-in that separates device and user identities. IT admins can migrate to Entra accounts easily with the migration wizard, and track progress using a dashboard in the Pro Management portal.

items.title: SharePoint: Updates to SharePoint home sites
items.description: SharePoint home sites are getting the following new updates - A resources web part, a new UX to customize the SharePoint app (Viva Connections) experience for Teams desktop and mobile and ability to set up a new home site in the SharePoint admin center. Additionally, the Announcements web part and a new layout for News web part will be available in all sites.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Copilot connectors are available for U.S. Department of Defense environment
items.description: Copilot connectors enable organizations to integrate content from  other external data sources into Microsoft Graph, improving Copilot’s intelligence, contextual responses, and content discoverability

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Surveys Agent and Copilot Chat in Microsoft Forms
items.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat is now integrated in Microsoft Forms, including enabling the Surveys Agent from within Forms. Surveys Agent provides specialized capabilities to help with your form - from recommending and applying improvements to your form or quiz, to helping draft and send invitations, to analyzing your results. Surveys Agent is available to users with a Microsoft 365 Copilot license, and can be accessed by clicking the "Copilot" icon in Forms.

items.title: SharePoint: Microsoft 365 Archive in GCC-L
items.description: Microsoft 365 Archive is designed to help organizations manage their inactive data efficiently. It allows you to move inactive SharePoint sites into a cold storage tier, which reduces storage costs while maintaining the same searchability, security, and compliance standards as active data. This feature is already available in Production and is now being released to GCC-L cloud.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Insider Risk Management- Insider risk management for agents
items.description:
As AI agents become deeply embedded in enterprise ecosystems, they are evolving beyond simple tools or workflows into a digital workforce. These agents can interpret intent, access and manipulate enterprise data, execute actions and even make real-time decisions. In many ways, they operate like human insiders only with machine-speed data processing capabilities. 

To govern and protect these agents effectively, Microsoft Purview Insider Risk Management is being expanded to agents, with specific

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Insider Risk Management-Insider risk management for agents
items.description: As AI agents become deeply embedded in enterprise ecosystems, they are evolving beyond simple tools or workflows into a digital workforce. These agents can interpret intent, access and manipulate enterprise data, execute actions and even make real-time decisions. In many ways, they operate like human insiders only with machine-speed data processing capabilities. To govern and protect these agents effectively, Microsoft Purview Insider Risk Management is being expanded to agents, with specific in

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Admins can manage access more precisely with support for nested permissions in Jira, Confluence, and ServiceNow
items.description: Support for hierarchical ACLs for SNOW, Confluence, Jira connectors.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Information Protection- New Summary Metrics in Content Explorer for Unscanned Files and Classification History Details
items.description:
Enhance Content Explorer by introducing new summary metrics that provide better visibility into scanning and classification activities. This update will include:
1. Unscanned Files Summary
Displays the count and percentage of files that have not been scanned for classification, helping admins identify gaps and take corrective action.
2. Classification History Insights
Shows trends and details of classification changes over time, helping admins to discover the stale data in their organization

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Engage private content in M365 Copilot
items.description: Engage content from private communities and events will be used as grounding source for M365 Copilot. When a user queries in M365 Copilot, they will only be able to see content they are privileged to access.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Export Copilot metrics in the Copilot Dashboard
items.description: This enables users to export row-level Copilot metrics from the Copilot Dashboard for reporting and deeper analysis.

items.title: Microsoft 365 admin center: Organizational Messages now supports Action Segments
items.description: Action Segments empowers IT Admins to target Organizational Messages using dynamic, pre-defined usage segments. This behavior-based targeting approach goes beyond existing static group definitions, ensuring messages reach the most relevant users.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Content Sources in Copilot Chat
items.description: Users will able to select content sources on Copilot Chat. When users select content sources the responses generated will be limited to content from the selected sources.

items.title: Outlook: add attachments while offline in the new Outlook for Windows
items.description: We are adding more functionality to offline mode in the new Outlook for Windows. You will now be able to add attachments while drafting your email without an internet connection. #newoutlookforwindows

items.title: Microsoft Purview compliance portal: Scanner data export for custom reporting
items.description: Learn about the state of your scanned repositories and create custom reports with the information protection scanner. Labeling, classification and protection actions on scanned files are stored in SQL tables that form a foundation to feed into custom reports.

items.title: Microsoft Purview compliance portal: Data Loss Prevention for endpoints - Optical character recognition (OCR) support for embedded images in endpoint
items.description: This release will extend OCR support from standalone images (JPEG, JPG, PNG, BMP, TIFF, and PDF) to images embedded inside the following files and file types: Office files (XLSX, DOCX, PPTX), container files (zip, rar, 7z, and more), and PDF files. Image-only PDF files are already supported, and this this release will support hybrid PDF files containing images and searchable text. Updated May 20, 2026: We have paused rollout and will resume soon. Thank you for your patience.

items.title: Microsoft Purview compliance portal: Data Loss Prevention - Optical character recognition (OCR) support for endpoint
items.description: Optical character recognition (OCR) support will detect sensitive content in images and subsequently apply data loss prevention (DLP) policies to prevent exfiltration of that sensitive data on your Windows endpoint devices. This release supports key file types like JPG, JPEG, PNG, TIFF, BMP and PDF (image only PDF).
